message:
fix: probe Kitty graphics support

- Add Kitty graphics query (a=q) probe via /dev/tty
- Use probe for robust inline protocol detection
- Keep Ghostty + Kitty fast-paths
- Update changelog

files.patch:
@@ -9,6 +9,7 @@
 - TUI: `f` (reveal) now auto-downloads the selected GIF if needed, then reveals it.
 - TUI: avoid emitting Kitty graphics sequences on unsupported terminals (no more base64 spew).
 - TUI: when inline images aren’t supported, exit with a helpful “supported terminals / protocol” message.
+- Inline detection: use Kitty graphics query (a=q) when available to avoid false positives.
 
 ### Dev
 - Replace pnpm workflow with `make` + npm (`make snap`, `make gifgrep ...`).

@@ -96,11 +96,11 @@ func thumbsProtocol(opts model.Options, stdout io.Writer, format outputFormat) t
 	case thumbsNever:
 		return termcaps.InlineNone
 	case thumbsAlways:
-		return termcaps.DetectInline(os.Getenv)
+		return termcaps.DetectInlineRobust(os.Getenv)
 	case thumbsAuto:
-		return termcaps.DetectInline(os.Getenv)
+		return termcaps.DetectInlineRobust(os.Getenv)
 	}
-	return termcaps.DetectInline(os.Getenv)
+	return termcaps.DetectInlineRobust(os.Getenv)
 }
 
 func renderPlain(
